prompt_text: Best HR that works with Slack and Google Workspace
response:
The **best HR tools that work with both Slack and Google Workspace** tend to fall into three buckets: full HRIS platforms, HR helpdesk/automation tools inside Slack, and focused add‑ons (time off, engagement, etc.). Below are strong options that support *both* Slack and Google Workspace, plus how to narrow them down.

---

### 1. Full HR / People Ops platforms with Slack + Google Workspace

These give you an HR “system of record” plus native or marketplace integrations:

- **BambooHR**  
  - Type: Core HRIS (employee records, PTO, onboarding, performance).  
  - Integrations: Listed among HR apps that integrate with **Google Workspace** via the Workspace Marketplace.[3] Supports Slack via connectors and third‑party bots (time‑off notifications, approvals, etc.).  
  - Best for: Small–mid sized companies wanting an all‑in‑one HRIS and willing to wire up Slack via add‑ons or lightweight integration.

- **Rippling** *(inferred from general market knowledge)*  
  - Type: All‑in‑one HR, IT, payroll, and device management.  
  - Integrations: Deep Google Workspace integration (account provisioning, group management) plus Slack workflows and bots for approvals and notifications.  
  - Best for: Fast‑growing startups or SMBs that want HR + IT + apps provisioning centrally managed and highly automated.

- **HiBob (bob)** *(inferred)*  
  - Type: Modern HRIS (core HR, performance, engagement).  
  - Integrations: Google Workspace SSO + directory sync; Slack app for kudos, announcements, and workflows.  
  - Best for: Distributed/modern teams that want strong engagement features embedded in Slack.

- **Deel / Gusto / Paylocity / Personio** *(inferred)*  
  - Type: Payroll‑centric all‑in‑one HR with growing HRIS capabilities.  
  - Integrations: Typically support Google Workspace SSO and have Slack notifications or basic bots for time‑off, onboarding tasks, and approvals.  
  - Best for: When payroll compliance is key and you only need “good enough” Slack workflows.

Because there is no single “best” across all companies, a practical approach is:

- If you **don’t yet have an HR system of record**, prioritize a full HRIS (BambooHR, Rippling, HiBob) that supports:  
  - Google Workspace SSO + directory sync  
  - Slack notifications + basic actions (approve time off, complete tasks)  
- If you **already have an HRIS**, you may get more ROI by adding a Slack‑first HR helpdesk or specific apps below instead of switching HRIS.

---

### 2. Slack‑first HR helpdesk & automation (on top of existing systems)

These live *inside Slack* and typically connect to your HRIS, Google Workspace, or ticketing tools to serve employees where they already work.

According to a 2026 roundup of **HR Slack helpdesks**, top tools include:[9]

- **ClearFeed**[9]  
  - Focus: Turn Slack HR questions/DMs into trackable tickets, add AI answers, route to the right HR owners.  
  - Integrations: Works with Slack; can integrate with external HR/ticketing platforms for deeper workflows.[9]  
  - Best for: HR teams overwhelmed by Slack DMs who want structured triage and self‑service.

- **Grok / Dasha / Polly / others in the list**[9]  
  - Focus: Variants of HR Q&A, surveys, and ticketing from Slack.  
  - Integrations: Most support SSO via Google Workspace or sync with Google Drive/Docs for knowledge base storage.

Choose this category if:

- Your **biggest pain is HR support volume in Slack** (repeated questions, lost DMs).  
- You want to keep your existing HRIS/payroll but **add a Slack “front door”** for HR.

---

### 3. Focused HR utilities that support Slack + Google Workspace

These are lighter‑weight tools that integrate exceptionally well with Slack and also plug into Google Workspace.

- **AttendanceBot** (highlighted in the “Google Workspace for HR” category on G2)[2]  
  - Focus: Time off, time tracking, hybrid work, and scheduling from Slack or Microsoft 365.[2]  
  - Integrations:  
    - Deep **Slack** integration for time‑off requests, status changes, and shift tracking.[2]  
    - Appears in the **Google Workspace for HR** category, implying integration with Google Calendar and related tools for scheduling and availability.[2]  
  - Best for: Teams that mainly need PTO and time tracking tightly embedded in Slack and synced with Google Calendar.

- **HR & Legal apps in Google Workspace Marketplace**[3]
